zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret De Hindley於1270年出生在英國爾時，她的父親Adam Hindley29歲，母親Elizabeth Culcheth25歲。她和William de Radcliffe至少有4個兒子和6個女兒。她去世於1349年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾享年79歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hindley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): habitational name from Hindley (Lancashire) or possibly Hiendley (Yorkshire), both from Old English hind ‘hind, female deer’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret De Hindley was born in 1270, in Cheshire, England, her father, Adam Hindley, was 29 and her mother, Elizabeth Culcheth, was 25. She had at least 4 sons and 6 daughters with William de Radcliffe. She died in 1349, in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret De Hindley est née en 1270, à Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Adam Hindley, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Culcheth, avait 25 ans. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles avec William de Radcliffe. Elle est décédée en 1349, à Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret De Hindley 1270 in Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Adam Hindley 29 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Culcheth 25. Sie hatte mit William de Radcliffe mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb 1349 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret De Hindley è nata il nel 1270 nel Cheshire, England, sua padre, Adam Hindley, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Culcheth, aveva 25 anni. Lei avuto almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie con William de Radcliffe. Morì nel 1349, nel Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret De Hindleyが1270年にイギリスチェシュアイアで生まれた時，父Adam Hindleyは29歳，母Elizabeth Culchethは25歳であった。彼女はWilliam de Radcliffeとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1349年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアで79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret De Hindley(이)가 1270년 영국 체실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Adam Hindley(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Culcheth(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 William de Radcliffe(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1349년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret De Hindley родилась в 1270 г. в местности чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Adam Hindley было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Culcheth, 25. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей от William de Radcliffe. Она умерла в 1349 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret De Hindley nació en 1270, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Adam Hindley, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Culcheth, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas con William de Radcliffe. Murió en 1349, en Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 79 años.
